name: Beefsteak tomato carpaccio with green onion and ginger salsa
intro: Double or triple the salsa, if you like; it’s absolutely delicious spooned over all

ingredients.item: 1¼-inch/3cm piece of ginger, peeled and roughly chopped (1 tbsp) flaked sea salt
green onions, very thinly sliced (¾ cup/45g)
oz/400g beefsteak tomatoes (about 2, depending on size), sliced
green chile, seeded and  nely chopped

method: Put the ginger and ½ tsp  aked salt into a pestle and mortar and crush to a
ne paste. Transfer to a bowl with the green onions and stir to combine.
Put the oil in a small pan and place over low heat until just warm (you don’t want it to heat too much). Pour over the green onions and add 1 tsp of the vinegar. Stir and set aside.
Lay the tomatoes on a large platter, with the slices slightly overlapping.

Season with ¼ tsp of  aked salt and drizzle with the remaining 1 tsp of vinegar. Spoon the green onion and ginger salsa evenly over the tomatoes (or use your hands to better effect), scatter the chile and cilantro on top and
Pictured with Lima bean mash with muhammara (this page)
The chile paste will keep for 3 days in a sealed container in the fridge. Once assembled, the dish will keep in the fridge for up to 2 days.
